sections.paragraph:
διαπλανάω (s. πλανάω; ordinarily in a negative sense: PGissUniv 1, 11f [II b.c.]; PBrem 12, 13 [II a.d.]; Arrian; Plut., Mor. 917e; Epict. 1, 20, 10 ‘mislead’; in pap e.g. Sb 5242, 8 [I a.d.]; BGU 36, 7; 436, 7; PRyl 125, 20 ‘prevaricate, deceive’) perh. to cause a diversion (without neg. sense, as Photius, MPG CIII 536C) amuse, entertain GJs 6:1 (on the variants s. Tdf., and deStrycker p. 303).

PGissUniv PGissUniv = Mitteilungen aus der Papyrussammlung der Giessener Universitätsbibliothek—List 4

PBrem PBrem = Die Bremer Papyri—List 4

Arrian Arrian = Flavius Arrianus, I–II a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

perh. perh. = perhaps

neg. neg. = negative

MPG MPG = Migne, Patrologia Graeca—List 5

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

deStrycker deStrycker = ÉdeStrycker; s. GJs—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 235
